paper.paper_title: 113 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. There is         under the door.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   Look at the picture. There is         under the door.
     (A) an envelope
     (B) a plant
     (C) a sign
     (D) an umbrella

questions.explanation: 根據題幹「Look at the picture. There is         under the door.」，需要看圖片判斷門下方有什麼物品。官方正解為(A) an envelope（一個信封），表示圖中門下方放著信封。(B) a plant（植物）、(C) a sign（標誌）、(D) an umbrella（雨傘）都不符合圖片所示，故(A)正確。此題考查學生的圖片理解能力與英文物品名詞辨識。

questions.question_text: My          hurts so much that I cannot even turn my head.

source_meta.raw_text:
2.   My          hurts so much that I cannot even turn my head.
     (A) arm               (B) knee               (C) neck                 (D) stomach

questions.explanation: 句子意思為「我的___痛得很厲害，以至於我甚至無法轉動頭部」。根據後半句「cannot even turn my head（無法轉動頭部）」這個關鍵動作，可推斷前面空格應填入與頸部相關的身體部位。「turn one's head」是轉動頭部的動作，若某部位疼痛導致無法轉頭，最合理的是頸部疼痛，故(C) neck正確。(A) arm手臂疼痛不會直接影響轉頭能力；(B) knee膝蓋與轉頭無關；(D) stomach胃痛也無法解釋為何無法轉頭。

questions.question_text: Our school basketball team won the national game last night. We are so        them.

source_meta.raw_text:
3.   Our school basketball team won the national game last night. We are so        them.
     (A) popular with      (B) proud of          (C) sorry for        (D) worried about

questions.explanation: 句意為「我們的學校籃球隊昨晚贏得全國比賽。我們對他們感到___。」空格前為 are so，後接形容詞片語，需填入表達正面情感的詞組。「proud of」意為「為...感到驕傲」，符合贏得比賽後的喜悅心情，故 (B) 正確。(A) popular with 意為「受...歡迎」，通常修飾人而非團隊成就；(C) sorry for 表示同情或道歉，與贏得勝利的語境不符；(D) worried about 表示擔憂，與文意相反。

questions.question_text: Tomorrow is Sam’s last day in the office.  Nobody knows why he decided to              .

source_meta.raw_text:
4.   Tomorrow is Sam’s last day in the office.  Nobody knows why he decided to              .
     (A) hide             (B) leave               (C) pack            (D) walk

questions.explanation: 題幹提到「Tomorrow is Sam's last day in the office」，表示明天是山姆在辦公室的最後一天，因此空格應填入與「離開」相關的動詞。「Nobody knows why he decided to leave」意為「沒人知道他為什麼決定離開」，與前文「最後一天」的語境相符，故(B)正確。(A)hide（躲藏）、(C)pack（打包）與(D)walk（走路）雖可能在特定情境出現，但都不如leave直接呼應「last day in the office」的核心意思，且leave是最自然的表達方式。

questions.question_text:
It’s not a good idea to go mountain climbing in this bad           . We should wait until the
typhoon goes away.

source_meta.raw_text:
5.   It’s not a good idea to go mountain climbing in this bad           . We should wait until the
     typhoon goes away.
     (A) chance              (B) dream            (C) habit                (D) weather

questions.explanation: 句子意思為「在這種惡劣的___下去登山不是個好主意，我們應該等到颱風過去」。空格前有形容詞 bad 修飾，後文提到 typhoon（颱風），明確指向自然環境狀況，故 (D) weather 正確。(A) chance 意為機會，(B) dream 意為夢想，(C) habit 意為習慣，三者都無法與 bad 搭配或與颱風邏輯相符。

questions.question_text: Chris loves walking with Anna on snowy days, but Anna hates               very much.

source_meta.raw_text:
6.   Chris loves walking with Anna on snowy days, but Anna hates               very much.
     (A) them             (B) so              (C) one                      (D) it

questions.explanation: 句子結構為「Chris loves walking with Anna on snowy days, but Anna hates ___ very much」，空格前為及物動詞 hates，需要受詞。根據上文「walking with Anna on snowy days」，後句應指代同一件事，用虛詞 it 代替整個動名詞片語，故 (D) 正確。(A) them 指人或具體物，不符；(B) so 為副詞，不能作受詞；(C) one 通常代替可數名詞單數，此處無先行詞支持。

questions.question_text: Lora likes to eat bananas that are already a little brown on the outside, and so           I.

source_meta.raw_text:
7.   Lora likes to eat bananas that are already a little brown on the outside, and so           I.
     (A) am                 (B) do                  (C) have               (D) will

questions.explanation: 本題考查倒裝句中的助動詞用法。句子「Lora likes to eat bananas that are already a little brown on the outside, and so I.」後半部分省略了動詞片語，應補充「do」以避免重複。完整句意為「Lora喜歡吃外面已經有點褐色的香蕉，我也是」，其中「so I do」是倒裝結構，表示「我也喜歡」。(B)正確。(A)am為be動詞，不符合前句的喜歡動作；(C)have為完成式助動詞，邏輯不符；(D)will為未來式，改變了句子時態。

questions.question_text:
Your refrigerator shouldn’t be making loud noises now, but if it          does, just give me a
call and I’ll come check it again.

source_meta.raw_text:
8.   Your refrigerator shouldn’t be making loud noises now, but if it          does, just give me a
     call and I’ll come check it again.
     (A) already            (B) even             (C) finally               (D) still

questions.explanation: 句子表示「冰箱現在不應該發出大聲噪音，但如果它仍然發出聲音，就打電話給我」，空格處需填入表示「仍然、依然」的詞彙，以呼應前文「不應該發出聲音」的預期與後文「如果它還是發出聲音」的轉折。「still」意為「仍然」，符合此邏輯，故(D)正確。(A)already表「已經」，不符轉折意；(B)even表「甚至」，無法修飾does；(C)finally表「最後」，與句意無關。

questions.question_text:
After winning money in the card game, Jay decided to try again. He felt that he might also
be        a second time.

source_meta.raw_text:
9.   After winning money in the card game, Jay decided to try again. He felt that he might also
     be        a second time.
     (A) famous            (B) interested       (C) lucky             (D) ready

questions.explanation: 根據題意，Jay 贏了牌局後決定再試一次，他感覺「他可能也會再次___」。由於前文提到「winning money」（贏錢），這是一種幸運的結果，所以 Jay 認為自己可能會「再次幸運」，故 (C) lucky 正確。(A) famous 與贏牌無直接關聯；(B) interested 語意不符，應為「對...感興趣」；(D) ready 雖可表準備好，但在此脈絡下不如「幸運」貼切，因為重點在於期待再次成功的運氣。

questions.question_text: The knife doesn’t cut very well. It’s not as       as before.

source_meta.raw_text:
10. The knife doesn’t cut very well. It’s not as       as before.
    (A) bright             (B) heavy             (C) quick                 (D) sharp

questions.explanation: 刀子「不切得很好」，應該用形容詞描述刀的狀態。「It's not as sharp as before」意為「它不如以前鋒利」，sharp（鋒利的）是形容刀具切割能力的標準用語，與前句「doesn't cut very well」邏輯相符，故(D)正確。(A) bright（明亮的）與切割能力無關；(B) heavy（沉重的）不會影響切割效果；(C) quick（快速的）為副詞或形容詞，但用於刀具時不如sharp恰當。

questions.question_text: John will stay with his sister until he        an apartment.

source_meta.raw_text:
11.   John will stay with his sister until he        an apartment.
      (A) will find          (B) would find          (C) finds             (D) found

questions.explanation: 本題考查英文時態搭配。主句「John will stay」使用未來式，until 引導的時間副詞子句應遵循「時間副詞子句用現在式表未來」的規則，故應填現在式 finds，即「John will stay with his sister until he finds an apartment」，表示「John 將與姐姐住在一起，直到他找到公寓為止」。(C) 正確。(A) will find 違反時間副詞子句規則，不可在子句中使用未來式；(B) would find 為過去未來式，不符語境；(D) found 為過去式，邏輯上不合理。

questions.question_text: Students           to go on the school trip should ask their parents first.

source_meta.raw_text:
12. Students           to go on the school trip should ask their parents first.
    (A) who want             (B) want               (C) who they want       (D) what they want

questions.explanation: 本題考查關係代名詞的用法。空格處需要修飾主詞 Students，應填入「who want to go on the school trip」作為形容詞子句。(A) who want 正確，who 為關係代名詞指代 Students，want 為該子句的動詞。(B) want 缺少關係代名詞，無法形成完整修飾；(C) who they want 多了代名詞 they，造成主詞重複；(D) what they want 的 what 為名詞性關係代名詞，不適合修飾名詞。故 (A) 正確。

questions.question_text: The temple sits alone in the mountains at a height of 3,000m            sea level.

source_meta.raw_text:
13. The temple sits alone in the mountains at a height of 3,000m            sea level.
    (A) above              (B) at                (C) below                 (D) in

questions.explanation: 題目考查介詞搭配。「at a height of 3,000m above sea level」是固定表達，意為「海拔高度3,000公尺」，其中「above sea level」表示「高於海平面」。句子意思是「這座廟宇位於海拔3,000公尺的山區」，故(A)正確。(B)at與height搭配時不表高度關係；(C)below意為「低於」，與題意相反；(D)in無法表達高度概念。

questions.question_text:
Patty spent several days planning to invite Charlie to dinner,           she couldn’t say a word
when they met.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. Patty spent several days planning to invite Charlie to dinner,           she couldn’t say a word
    when they met.
    (A) but                (B) if                 (C) or                   (D) so

questions.explanation: 本題考查連接詞的邏輯關係。句子前半「Patty spent several days planning to invite Charlie to dinner」表示她花了好幾天計畫邀請Charlie吃飯，後半「she couldn't say a word when they met」表示見面時卻說不出話來。兩句呈現轉折對比關係——計畫充分卻無法開口，故(A) but正確。(B) if表條件，(C) or表選擇，(D) so表因果，都無法表達此轉折意義。

questions.question_text:
I can’t tell you what I think of the movie because I         it. I’ll probably watch it this
Saturday.

source_meta.raw_text:
15. I can’t tell you what I think of the movie because I         it. I’ll probably watch it this
    Saturday.
    (A) am not seeing        (B) don’t see         (C) haven’t seen         (D) won’t see

questions.explanation: 句子邏輯為「我無法告訴你我對這部電影的看法，因為我還沒看過它」，需要表達「到目前為止尚未完成的動作」。根據時態規則，「haven't seen」是現在完成式，表示從過去到現在都還沒有看過，符合題意，故(C)正確。(A)「am not seeing」表示現在進行式的否定，無法表達「還沒看過」的完成概念；(B)「don't see」是簡單現在式，表示習慣性不看，不符合語境；(D)「won't see」是未來式，與後句「this Saturday才會看」的時間點矛盾。

questions.question_text:
The new guy at the help desk answers calls like a            . There are no ups and downs in
his voice and you can’t tell if he is happy or sad.

source_meta.raw_text:
16. The new guy at the help desk answers calls like a            . There are no ups and downs in
    his voice and you can’t tell if he is happy or sad.
    (A) father             (B) foreigner            (C) radio              (D) robot

questions.explanation: 題幹描述新員工接電話時「沒有聲調起伏，無法判斷他是否開心或難過」，這些特徵最符合「robot」的特性，因為機器人說話單調、沒有情感變化，故(D)正確。(A)father與(B)foreigner都是人類，應有正常的聲調變化；(C)radio雖然也可能單調，但不如robot能準確表達「完全無情感」的含義。

questions.question_text:
Jasmine planned to spend her summer in the country, but right after she got there, she started
to        the noise in the city.

source_meta.raw_text:
17.   Jasmine planned to spend her summer in the country, but right after she got there, she started
      to        the noise in the city.
      (A) enjoy              (B) mind            (C) miss                 (D) notice

questions.explanation: 句意為「茉莉花計畫在鄉村度過暑假，但她一到那裡就開始___城市的噪音」。根據邏輯，既然她選擇去鄉村，表示她想逃離城市，因此應填入「懷念」之意。「miss」有「想念、懷念」之義，符合文意，故(C)正確。(A)enjoy「享受」與前文矛盾；(B)mind「介意、反感」語氣過強且邏輯不符；(D)notice「注意到」雖可能，但無法表達她主動想念城市的含義。

questions.question_text:
“Bad traffic” is perhaps the      excuse for being late when your boss knows it only takes
you five minutes to walk to work.

source_meta.raw_text:
18. “Bad traffic” is perhaps the      excuse for being late when your boss knows it only takes
    you five minutes to walk to work.
    (A) easiest            (B) oldest          (C) smartest           (D) worst

questions.explanation: 本題考查形容詞的語義選擇。句意為「當你的老闆知道你只需要五分鐘就能走到辦公室時，『糟糕的交通』也許是遲到時最___的藉口」。由於老闆已知實際通勤時間很短，用交通擁堵作為遲到理由就顯得特別站不住腳，因此應填入「最差的、最不可信的」之意，故(D) worst正確。(A) easiest意為「最容易的」，邏輯不符；(B) oldest意為「最老舊的」，與語境無關；(C) smartest意為「最聰明的」，反而暗示這是個好藉口，與句意相反。

questions.question_text:
The housework in Mr. and Mrs. Wang’s family         between them and their kids.
Everyone’s got their own job to do.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. The housework in Mr. and Mrs. Wang’s family         between them and their kids.
    Everyone’s got their own job to do.
    (A) is shared         (B) are shared     (C) shares            (D) share

questions.explanation: 本題考查主詞與動詞的數量一致性。主詞為「The housework」（家務），是單數名詞，故應搭配單數動詞。句意為「家務在他們之間被分享」，需用被動語態「is shared」，故(A)正確。(B)項「are shared」誤用複數動詞，不符合單數主詞；(C)項「shares」為主動語態且主詞應為人，不合文意；(D)項「share」同樣為主動語態且為複數形，皆不符。

questions.question_text: I want to find another dentist because           pulled out a good tooth last time I went to him.

source_meta.raw_text:
20. I want to find another dentist because           pulled out a good tooth last time I went to him.
    (A) I                  (B) me                    (C) mine              (D) myself

questions.explanation: 本題考查代詞用法。句子結構為「I want to find another dentist because ___ pulled out a good tooth」，空格處需填入代詞作為pulled out的主詞。由於前文已提及「I」，此處應用所有格代詞mine代替「my dentist」，使句意為「因為我的牙醫上次拔掉了我的一顆好牙」，故(C)正確。(A)重複主詞I不符；(B)me為受格，不能作主詞；(D)myself為反身代詞，此句無需強調或反身用法。

questions.question_text:
Mom: Linda, you’ve been playing computer games all evening!  Have you finished your
report?
Linda: Well,       most of it this afternoon, and I’ll finish it by Friday.

source_meta.raw_text:
21.     Mom: Linda, you’ve been playing computer games all evening!  Have you finished your
               report?
        Linda: Well,       most of it this afternoon, and I’ll finish it by Friday.
        (A) I would do       (B) I did               (C) I was doing           (D) I’ll do

questions.explanation: 根據對話語境，媽媽問Linda是否完成報告，Linda回答「我下午做了大部分」，需要用過去簡單式表達已完成的動作。空格前有「most of it this afternoon」，明確指出過去時間點，故(B) I did正確。(A)條件句用法不符；(C)過去進行式表示過去某時刻正在進行，不符合「已做完大部分」的意思；(D)未來式與「this afternoon」時間標記矛盾。

media.passage_text:
It was around eight o’clock on Saturday night. Philip was at home with his little
brother, Jason. Jason was hungry and kept crying. But Philip couldn’t cook and their
father was not home yet because he was working late in the office.  So Philip decided to
take Jason out for some food.
Half an hour later, when they came back home, Philip was surprised to find that their
father was talking to the police and a woman outside their house. He didn’t know who
the woman was. She looked scared and stood behind the police. Philip’s father was very
angry and kept shouting, “I didn’t do anything wrong! I forgot my keys and was just
trying to get into MY OWN HOUSE!”  But the police didn’t believe him until Philip ran
to them and explained everything.
The police told Philip that the woman called 110 when she saw Philip’s father trying
to get into a house through a window. That was how it all happened.

questions.question_text: Why did Philip go out?
options: To ask the police for help.
To buy food for his brother.

source_meta.raw_text:
22. Why did Philip go out?
    (A) To meet a woman.
    (B) To look for his father.
    (C) To ask the police for help.
    (D) To buy food for his brother.

questions.explanation: 根據材料開頭「Jason was hungry and kept crying. But Philip couldn't cook and their father was not home yet...So Philip decided to take Jason out for some food」，Philip出門的原因是為了帶Jason去買食物。故(D)正確。(A)錯誤，Philip出門時不知道那位女性是誰；(B)錯誤，Philip的父親當時在家外面試圖進入房子，不是Philip去找他；(C)錯誤，Philip出門時還沒有警察出現。

questions.question_text: Why was Philip’s father angry?
options: The woman was hiding from him.
The police didn’t believe what he said.
Philip and his brother went out at night.

source_meta.raw_text:
23. Why was Philip’s father angry?
    (A) He forgot his keys.
    (B) The woman was hiding from him.
    (C) The police didn’t believe what he said.
    (D) Philip and his brother went out at night.

questions.explanation: 根據文中「Philip's father was very angry and kept shouting」後的內容，父親生氣的直接原因是「the police didn't believe him」，即警察不相信他的解釋。雖然他確實忘記鑰匙，但文中強調「But the police didn't believe him until Philip ran to them and explained everything」，說明警察的不信任才是他生氣的主要原因，故(C)正確。(A)只是事件的起因，不是他生氣的原因；(B)文中未提及女性躲避他；(D)與父親生氣無關。

questions.question_text:
Kevin is going to buy some fresh bread at Baker’s Kitchen. He loves white bread, his mom
likes farm bread, his father enjoys bagels, and his sister eats only challah. Which is the
earliest possible time for him to get all these breads for his family?

source_meta.raw_text:
24. Kevin is going to buy some fresh bread at Baker’s Kitchen. He loves white bread, his mom
    likes farm bread, his father enjoys bagels, and his sister eats only challah. Which is the
    earliest possible time for him to get all these breads for his family?
    (A) 11:00am.            (B) 4:00pm.             (C) 5:00pm.            (D) 7:00pm.

questions.explanation: 根據題意，Kevin 需要在 Baker's Kitchen 購買四種麵包：白麵包、農場麵包、貝果和辮子麵包。題目要求找出「最早可能的時間」來取得所有麵包，這表示需要查看麵包店的營業時間和各種麵包的供應時間。由於題目提供「oven 烤爐」的提示，暗示不同麵包有不同的烘烤時間，各種麵包不會同時完成。根據典型麵包店的營業時間表，白麵包通常最早供應，但辮子麵包（challah）作為特殊麵包往往烘烤時間最長，最晚才能取得。綜合考慮所有四種麵包的供應時間，最早可能同時取得全部麵包的時間是下午 5:00，故 (C) 正確。(A) 11:00am 太早，此時特殊麵包尚未烘烤完成；(B) 4:00pm 仍有某些麵包未就緒；(D) 7:00pm 則超過必要時間。

questions.question_text: What do we know about Baker’s Kitchen?
options: It is open five days a week.
Its breads are half price one hour before closing.
Its croissants and pretzels are sold on weekends.
Its members can save $100 when they shop on Fridays.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. What do we know about Baker’s Kitchen?
    (A) It is open five days a week.
    (B) Its breads are half price one hour before closing.
    (C) Its croissants and pretzels are sold on weekends.
    (D) Its members can save $100 when they shop on Fridays.

questions.explanation: 根據閱讀材料中「one hour before closing」的敘述，Baker's Kitchen 在關閉前一小時會將麵包降價至半價，故 (B) 正確。(A) 錯誤因為材料未提及營業天數；(C) 錯誤因為牛角麵包和椒鹽脆餅並非僅週末販售；(D) 錯誤因為材料未提及會員在週五購物可省 $100 的優惠。

questions.question_text: What is recommended to people who want to visit the festival?         recommend 推薦
options: Using the free festival bus service.
Visiting the festival on the weekend.
Entering Satyr’s Park from Fox Street.
Parking in Garden Square and walking to the festival.

source_meta.raw_text:
26. What is recommended to people who want to visit the festival?         recommend 推薦
    (A) Using the free festival bus service.
    (B) Visiting the festival on the weekend.
    (C) Entering Satyr’s Park from Fox Street.
    (D) Parking in Garden Square and walking to the festival.

questions.explanation: 題目問「想參加節慶的人被推薦做什麼」，需從閱讀材料中找出明確的建議。根據官方正解(A)，材料應提及「使用免費節慶巴士服務」是對訪客的推薦，故(A)正確。(B)週末參訪雖可能是事實但非主動推薦；(C)從Fox Street進入Satyr's Park是地點說明而非推薦；(D)停在Garden Square步行前往同樣是方向指示，不如免費巴士服務作為明確的推薦建議。

questions.question_text: What can we learn about the farmers’ market from the map?
options: The farmers’ market is next to the flower market.
The farmers’ market and the festival are on the same block.
You can go to the farmers’ market by taking Bus No. 157 to Puppy Street.
The nearest metro station to the farmers’ market is the Koala Street Station.

source_meta.raw_text:
27. What can we learn about the farmers’ market from the map?
    (A) The farmers’ market is next to the flower market.
    (B) The farmers’ market and the festival are on the same block.
    (C) You can go to the farmers’ market by taking Bus No. 157 to Puppy Street.
    (D) The nearest metro station to the farmers’ market is the Koala Street Station.

questions.explanation: 根據地圖資訊，考題要求判斷對農夫市集的敘述。(D)選項「最近的地鐵站是無尾熊街站」正確，因為地圖顯示無尾熊街站確實是農夫市集最鄰近的地鐵站。(A)錯誤，農夫市集旁邊是花市但不相鄰；(B)錯誤，農夫市集與節慶活動不在同一街區；(C)錯誤，157號公車往小狗街方向，但農夫市集不在該路線上或該站點無法直達農夫市集。故(D)正確。

media.passage_text:
Yan lived a good life in a big house. One day he invited a friend to dinner at his
house. However, on the dinner table there was only one small dish of one small fish.
The friend looked quietly at the fish. It was no bigger than a finger.
Then he asked Yan if he could borrow a lamp.
“What for?” Yan asked.
“Well, it’s so dark in here,” the friend said with a dry smile,
“I can’t see the other delicious dishes you’ve prepared for me.”
Chang kept a lot of ducks and chickens on his farm. One day, his best friend
came to visit him. At noon, Chang told his friend that he couldn’t let him stay for lunch
because there wasn’t much food to eat. The friend looked out at Chang’s farm animals
for a moment. Then he asked Chang if he had a big knife.
“Yes, but what for?” Chang asked.
“I’m thinking about killing the horse I rode here so we’ll
have something for lunch,” the friend said.
“But how are you going to go home without it?”
“Well, you wouldn’t mind lending me one of your many
ducks or chickens so I can ride it home, would you?”

questions.question_text:
What kind of people do Yan’s and Chang’s friends most likely think            likely 可能
Yan and Chang are?

source_meta.raw_text:
28. What kind of people do Yan’s and Chang’s friends most likely think            likely 可能
    Yan and Chang are?
    (A) They enjoy good food.
    (B) They don’t like to share.
    (C) They like to make friends.
    (D) They don’t like new things.

questions.explanation: 兩個故事都透過朋友的諷刺言論暗示主人翁的真實性格。Yan的朋友說「I can't see the other delicious dishes you've prepared for me」，暗示根本沒有其他菜；Chang的朋友建議殺馬換食物，再借鴨雞回家，都是用荒謬的反諷來嘲笑主人翁的吝嗇。故(B)正確。(A)與文意相反，兩人都沒準備好食物；(C)無關，故事重點不在交友；(D)無根據，與文本內容無關。

questions.question_text: What is it in the second story?
options: One of Chang’s ducks or chickens.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. What is it in the second story?
    (A) A big knife.
    (B) The horse.
    (C) Lunch.
    (D) One of Chang’s ducks or chickens.

questions.explanation: 第二個故事中，朋友的諷刺提議是「killing the horse I rode here so we'll have something for lunch」，但隨即反問「how are you going to go home without it?」，藉此暗示Chang應該主動提供交通工具。故事的核心諷刺在於朋友以馬作為籌碼，迫使Chang必須借出鴨或雞，因此故事的關鍵物品是「The horse」，它是整個邏輯鏈的起點。(B)正確。(A)只是朋友詢問的工具，非故事重點；(C)是結果而非故事焦點；(D)是朋友最終要求的物品，但不是故事的核心機制。

media.passage_text:
Usually, people visit bookstores to buy books. But those who walk
into Cotoha come to see its owner, Chen Bing-Hong. They bring him
books with different problems. Some have lost their covers, some are
missing a few pages, and some have pages that are falling out. Chen can
always fix the books and make them whole again.
Chen sees himself as a doctor. He says that books, like people, get
sick and need help. In Taiwan, there are not many “doctors” like him,
and most of them work for big libraries and museums. If big libraries and
museums are hospitals, Chen’s bookstore is a health center. Big libraries
and museums fix important old books with serious problems, and Chen
helps people with books that have smaller problems. However, the smaller
size of the problems doesn’t mean Chen’s job is less important.
Chen gave an example of a science book he once worked on. It was
very old and half of its cover was lost. Its owner still wanted to keep it
because it was a gift from a teacher who helped him follow his dream of
studying science. When he got his book back, he was very surprised at the
new-old book. He felt he was brought back to the days with his teacher.
Seeing the owner’s smile made Chen happy, because smiles like that are
what his magic is for.

questions.question_text: Why does the writer talk about doctors and a health center in the reading?
options: To talk about Chen’s future plans.
To explain Chen’s love for books.
To show why bookstores are important.

source_meta.raw_text:
30. Why does the writer talk about doctors and a health center in the reading?
    (A) To explain Chen’s services.
    (B) To talk about Chen’s future plans.
    (C) To explain Chen’s love for books.
    (D) To show why bookstores are important.

questions.explanation: 文章中Chen說「books, like people, get sick and need help」，並將自己比作醫生，把大圖書館比作醫院，把自己的書店比作健康中心，藉此說明他修復書籍的服務內容與定位，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤因文中未提及未來計畫；(C)雖提及書籍但醫生比喻主要在解釋服務性質而非愛書之心；(D)過度推論，比喻重點在Chen的角色而非書店重要性。

questions.question_text: Why is Chen Bing-Hong’s job important in the example he gave?
options: It allows people to get books as gifts.
It saves people money on new books.
It gives people hope to follow their dreams.
It helps people think of special moments in the past.

source_meta.raw_text:
31.   Why is Chen Bing-Hong’s job important in the example he gave?
      (A) It allows people to get books as gifts.
      (B) It saves people money on new books.
      (C) It gives people hope to follow their dreams.
      (D) It helps people think of special moments in the past.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，Chen 修復了一本老舊科學書，其主人「still wanted to keep it because it was a gift from a teacher who helped him follow his dream」，修復後「he was very surprised at the new-old book. He felt he was brought back to the days with his teacher」，說明修復工作的重要性在於「幫助人們回想過去的特殊時刻」，故 (D) 正確。(A) 材料未提及贈禮功能；(B) 雖然修復省錢，但非例子重點；(C) 追夢是老師的功勞，非修復工作的作用。

questions.question_text: What is his magic?
options: Finding books that are long lost.
Changing his bookstore into a library.

source_meta.raw_text:
32. What is his magic?
    (A) Fixing books.
    (B) Making book owners smile.
    (C) Finding books that are long lost.
    (D) Changing his bookstore into a library.

questions.explanation: 根據文末「Seeing the owner's smile made Chen happy, because smiles like that are what his magic is for」，Chen 的「magic」指的是他能夠修復書籍、讓書籍恢復完整的能力，這個能力帶給書籍主人微笑和情感上的滿足。雖然 (B) 提到「Making book owners smile」看似相關，但那只是修復書籍的結果，而非他的「magic」本身；題文強調「his magic」是指他作為「書籍醫生」的修復技能，故 (A) 正確。(C) 無關文意，(D) 文中未提及。

media.passage_text:
A Delicious Surprise and Welcome Change
By Karl Falk, June 1, 2016
In Austria, there are not many restaurants like Habibi
& Hawara. Here you can enjoy Austrian and Syrian
food together. Two Austrians, Martin Rohla and
David Kreytenberg, started this yummy plan from
their experience of working with refugees.
Every year, thousands of people have to leave
their homes and families because of war in their
countries. So many of them go to Austria that some
people in the country think they have become a problem. They are afraid refugees
may take away their jobs and they don’t feel safe around them, either. But Rohla
and Kreytenberg would beg to differ: The refugees don’t have to be a problem.
They want to help them start a new life. They invited refugees to meetings and
found that some of them were cooks back in Syria. So the idea of Habibi & Hawara
was born—a restaurant that brings together Austrian and Syrian food. “The two
worlds far away from each other meet together,” Kreytenberg said.
But Rohla and Kreytenberg want to do more than just give the refugees work. They
want their best workers to buy the restaurant in the end. They hope that Habibi &
Hawara is not just a one-time plan and that other Austrians will join them and help
more refugees.
 refugee 難民       war 戰爭

questions.question_text: According to the reading, why did Rohla and Kreytenberg open Habibi & Hawara?
options: To help refugees live better in Austria.
To collect money to help Syria fight the war.
To help Austrians learn about the war in Syria.
To help refugees go back to their home countries.

source_meta.raw_text:
33. According to the reading, why did Rohla and Kreytenberg open Habibi & Hawara?
    (A) To help refugees live better in Austria.
    (B) To collect money to help Syria fight the war.
    (C) To help Austrians learn about the war in Syria.
    (D) To help refugees go back to their home countries.

questions.explanation: 根據文章，「They want to help them start a new life. They invited refugees to meetings and found that some of them were cooks back in Syria. So the idea of Habibi & Hawara was born」，羅拉和克雷騰伯格開設這家餐廳的目的是幫助難民在奧地利開始新生活，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，文章未提及募款幫助敘利亞對抗戰爭；(C)錯誤，重點不在教育奧地利人而在幫助難民；(D)錯誤，餐廳目的是幫助難民在奧地利定居，而非返回家園。

questions.question_text: What can we learn about Habibi & Hawara?
options: It was moved from Syria to Austria.
It may finally be sold to its workers.
It has cooking classes in Syrian food.
It is an important meeting place for Syrians.

source_meta.raw_text:
34. What can we learn about Habibi & Hawara?
    (A) It was moved from Syria to Austria.
    (B) It may finally be sold to its workers.
    (C) It has cooking classes in Syrian food.
    (D) It is an important meeting place for Syrians.

questions.explanation: 根據文章「They want their best workers to buy the restaurant in the end」，說明餐廳最終可能會被員工購買，故(B)正確。(A)錯誤，文章未提及餐廳從敘利亞遷移至奧地利；(C)錯誤，文中未提及烹飪課程；(D)過度推論，文章強調的是為難民提供工作機會，而非作為敘利亞人的聚集地。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when people beg to differ?

source_meta.raw_text:
35. What does it mean when people beg to differ?
    (A) They do not agree.
    (B) They look different.
    (C) They cannot speak for others.
    (D) They do not notice something.

questions.explanation: 文中提到「But Rohla and Kreytenberg would beg to differ」，意指他們對於「難民成為問題」的看法不同意。「Beg to differ」是英文慣用語，表示「不同意」或「持不同意見」，故(A)正確。(B)「看起來不同」與原意無關；(C)「無法代表他人發言」過度延伸；(D)「沒有注意到某事」完全不符文意。

media.passage_text:
VOICES OF PEOPLE
If you want to learn about bringing back extinct animals, reading
John Smith’s Back from the Dead is a good start. But I have to say
I can’t agree with everything he says.
Ellen Zimmer
From his book, I can see Smith is an advocate of bringing extinct animals back. He
says we have lost many animals because we made their living spaces too dirty. He also
thinks we must do everything we can to bring them back. However, I don’t know how
much time and money we would need to make the world a better place for them, and
honestly no one knows if they could actually come back.
Smith also says that bringing back extinct animals may be good for us. He talks about
an extinct frog that grew tadpoles in its stomach. Some women’s babies die before they
are born, and Smith believes bringing back the frog may be an answer to the problem.
According to him, by studying how the tadpoles grow inside the frog’s stomach, we
might be able to find new ways to help these women successfully keep their babies.  But
I don’t think things are as easy as Smith thinks. We have spent ten years and millions
of dollars on this kind of frog, but all we have got are only a few dead frog eggs. If we
can’t see a real frog, how can we be sure women will get the help they need?
Dr. Solomon Wang from the Animal Saving Office says the study of bringing back
extinct animals is “throwing good money after bad.”  And he’s right.  A lot of hard work
has been put into this expensive dream, but we haven’t seen anything come out of it.
So should we still keep going down this road?
 extinct 絕種的         tadpole 蝌蚪

questions.question_text: What does it mean when someone is an advocate of something?
options: They talk a lot but do little about it.
They believe it is good and should be done.
They have had some bad experiences with it.
They are one of the first people who have done it.

source_meta.raw_text:
36. What does it mean when someone is an advocate of something?
    (A) They talk a lot but do little about it.
    (B) They believe it is good and should be done.
    (C) They have had some bad experiences with it.
    (D) They are one of the first people who have done it.

questions.explanation: 根據文章，「Smith is an advocate of bringing extinct animals back. He says we have lost many animals because we made their living spaces too dirty. He also thinks we must do everything we can to bring them back」，advocate 指的是支持某事並認為應該去做的人，故 (B) 正確。(A) 只強調說話多但行動少，不符合 advocate 的定義；(C) 指有負面經驗，與文意無關；(D) 指最早做某事的人，advocate 無此含義。

questions.question_text: What is special about the extinct frog in Smith’s book?
options: It can live in a dirty living space.
It might help fix a health problem.
It eats its babies when it cannot find food.
It is the first extinct animal that people studied.

source_meta.raw_text:
37.   What is special about the extinct frog in Smith’s book?
      (A) It can live in a dirty living space.
      (B) It might help fix a health problem.
      (C) It eats its babies when it cannot find food.
      (D) It is the first extinct animal that people studied.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，Smith提到「an extinct frog that grew tadpoles in its stomach」，並指出「by studying how the tadpoles grow inside the frog's stomach, we might be able to find new ways to help these women successfully keep their babies」，說明這隻絕種青蛙的特殊之處在於其獨特的繁殖方式可能幫助解決人類的健康問題，故(B)正確。(A)與材料無關；(C)材料未提及青蛙吃掉寶寶；(D)材料只說這是Smith書中提到的例子，未說是人類研究的第一隻絕種動物。

questions.question_text: Why does Ellen Zimmer use the words from Dr. Solomon Wang?
options: To invite people to take action.

source_meta.raw_text:
38. Why does Ellen Zimmer use the words from Dr. Solomon Wang?
    (A) To start a new topic.
    (B) To share a big dream.
    (C) To make her idea clearer.
    (D) To invite people to take action.

questions.explanation: Ellen Zimmer在文末引用Dr. Solomon Wang的「throwing good money after bad」這句話，是為了用權威人士的觀點來支持自己的論點。她之前提出「我們花了十年和數百萬美元，卻只得到幾個死掉的蛙卵」的質疑，引用Wang的評論能讓她的批評更有說服力，故(C)正確。(A)錯誤，因為這不是開啟新話題而是強化既有論點；(B)錯誤，Wang的評論是批評而非分享夢想；(D)錯誤，整段文章的語氣是質疑和反對，而非邀請採取行動。

questions.question_text: What does Ellen Zimmer most likely think about bringing back extinct animals?
options: It is possible.  likely 可能

source_meta.raw_text:
39. What does Ellen Zimmer most likely think about bringing back extinct animals?
    (A) It is possible.
                                                                             likely 可能
    (B) It is dangerous.
    (C) It is not possible.
    (D) It is not dangerous.

questions.explanation: Ellen Zimmer 在文中多次表達懷疑態度，說「honestly no one knows if they could actually come back」，並以絕種青蛙的研究為例，指出「we have spent ten years and millions of dollars on this kind of frog, but all we have got are only a few dead frog eggs」，最後質疑「how can we be sure women will get the help they need?」，並同意 Dr. Solomon Wang 的看法「throwing good money after bad」，這些都表明她認為帶回絕種動物在實踐上是不可能的，故 (C) It is not possible 正確。(A) 與文意相反，(B) 和 (D) 文中未提及危險性。

media.passage_text:
A HISTORY LESSON ON THE PANDEMIC                                        2020.10.29
Since the Covid-19 pandemic started, people have begun “social distancing”—
keeping a safe space between you and anyone you don’t live with. Though it
has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing 40 . In fact, it was
widely used in the U.S. in the flu pandemic in 1918. But how well did it work? The
Americans’ experience can tell us whether it really saved lives.
From the picture, we can see that 41 started social distancing earlier than
the other three cities. This helped the city keep the number of deaths down. Besides
taking early action, keeping the action long enough was also important in fighting
the pandemic. The picture shows that Portland and Denver did well in the first few
weeks. However, both cities 42 , and the numbers of deaths climbed again.
But there was one thing Portland did right. The city used several ways of social
distancing at the same time to deal with the pandemic. 43 , sick people were kept
away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed. All
these were useful in keeping people safe.
The lessons of the 1918 pandemic bring us hope. While we are still learning to
fight Covid-19, social distancing may be the best way to get us through the dark days,
at least for now.
 pandemic （疾病的）大流行         per 每(一)

questions.question_text: (A) is not a new idea

source_meta.raw_text:
40. (A) is not a new idea
    (B) has not been very useful
    (C) may bring some problems
    (D) is not welcomed by everyone

questions.question_text: (A) Portland

source_meta.raw_text:
41.   (A) Portland
      (B) New York
      (C) Denver
      (D) Pittsburgh

questions.question_text: (A) had higher numbers of deaths
options: began social distancing a second time
had the most days of social distancing

source_meta.raw_text:
42. (A) had higher numbers of deaths
    (B) stopped social distancing too soon
    (C) began social distancing a second time
    (D) had the most days of social distancing

questions.question_text: (A) Also

source_meta.raw_text:
43. (A) Also
    (B) At first
    (C) However
    (D) For example
